compiled_state.title: review-complexity-classifier.sh fails with exit 127 via dso shim
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Bug Description

review-complexity-classifier.sh fails with exit 127 when invoked via the dso shim (`.claude/scripts/dso review-complexity-classifier.sh`).

## Context

During sprint execution of epic 8fdd-a993, the REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md Step 3 runs the classifier to determine review tier (light/standard/deep). The classifier is invoked via the dso shim path.

## Actions Taken

In batch 2's commit workflow, I ran:
```bash
CLASSIFIER_OUTPUT=$(".claude/scripts/dso review-complexity-classifier.sh" < "$DIFF_FILE" 2>/dev/null) || CLASSIFIER_EXIT=$?
```

## Observed Output

```
Classifier failed (exit 127) — defaulting to standard
```

Exit 127 means 'command not found' — the dso shim could not locate the script. The script exists at `plugins/dso/scripts/review-complexity-classifier.sh` but the shim resolution failed.

## Reasoning

I accepted the default-to-standard fallback per the classifier failure contract in REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md and moved on. I did not investigate WHY the shim failed (e.g., whether the script name doesn't match the shim's lookup pattern, or whether the shim only resolves a subset of scripts). This was a mistake — I should have tried the direct path (`plugins/dso/scripts/review-complexity-classifier.sh`) as a diagnostic step.

## Expected Behavior

`.claude/scripts/dso review-complexity-classifier.sh` should resolve to `plugins/dso/scripts/review-complexity-classifier.sh` and execute it.

## Reproduction

```bash
echo 'some diff content' | .claude/scripts/dso review-complexity-classifier.sh
# Expected: JSON classifier output
# Actual: exit 127 (command not found)
```
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
